case_title: NETLINE CORPORATION, VS. LOOMIS GROUP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOS. TO APPLICATION FOR ISSUANCE OF RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT LOOMIS GROUP, INC.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER; ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT; APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 11, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. PRO TEM JUDGE: KATHERINE HUIBONHOA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER; ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT - APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION SUBMITTED SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH ITS PRIMA FACIE CASE, INCLUDING EVIDENCE THAT LOOMIS GROUP ACKNOWLEDGED THE DEBT. DEFENDANT LOOMIS GROUP DID NOT OBJECT TO THIS EVIDENCE AND FAILED TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIMED DEFENSES WITH ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS THE DECLARATION OF JEFFREY LOOMIS DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION AND SUPPORTING EVIDENCE ARE SUFFICIENT TO SATISFY THE "PROBABLE VALIDITY" STANDARD. PLAINTIFF TO POST A $10,000 BOND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

IN DEPARTMENT 301 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. NO LATER THAN 4 P.M., JANUARY 4, 2013, THE MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE OF JANUARY 14, 2013, OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. DECLARATION OF PATRICK M.K. RICHARDSON REGARDING PLTF'S MTN FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR ORDER THAT THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER SPECIFIEDIN REQ.'S FOR ADMISSION BE DEEMED ADMITED FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES, COSTS AND FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT LOOMIS GROUP, INC.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR ORDER THAT THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER SPECIFIED IN REQUEST'S FOR ADMISSION BE DEEMED ADMITTED, FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES, COSTS, AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. IT IS HEREBY ORDERED THAT THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET TWO ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. IN LIGHT OF THIS RULING, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS IT RELATES TO ITS FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, -- WHICH ONLY ASK FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17.0 - IS MOOT, AS DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET TWO ARE NOW ALL DEEMED UNQUALIFIED ADMISSIONS. DEFENDANT IS FURTHER ORDERED TO PAY SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $935.00 (2.5 HOURS AT $350.00 PER HOUR PLUS $60.00 FILING FEE) ON OR BEFORE JANUARY 24, 2013. ORDER SIGNED. PRO TEM JUDGE: ANTHONY SCHOENBERG, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO ADD ALTER EGO JUDGMENT DEBTOR IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 3, 2013 TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 27, 2013 AT 4:00 P.M. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-03-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO ADD JEFFREY LOOMIS AS AN ALTER EGO IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT JEFFREY LOOMIS HAD CONTROL OVER THE LITIGATION OF JUDGMENT DEBTOR. (SEE NEC ELECTRONICS INC. V. HURT, 208 CAL. APP. 3D 772, 778 (1989); DECLARATION OF JONICH, 5-6; DECLARATION OF RICHARDSON IN OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO ADD ALTER EGO JUDGMENT DEBTOR, 3-9.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRODUCED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO MEET THE MULTI-FACTOR ALTER EGO TEST THAT THERE WAS A UNITY OF INTEREST BETWEEN JEFFREY LOOMIS AND JUDGMENT DEBTOR, AND THAT INEQUITABLE RESULTS WILL FOLLOW IF THE CORPORATE SEPARATENESS IS RESPECTED. (SEE ZORAN CORP. V. CHEN, 185 CAL. APP. 4TH 799, 811 (2010).) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Alter Ego Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Sep-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION's Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Jeffrey Loomis as an alter ego is denied. Plaintiff has not established that Jeffrey Loomis had control over the litigation of judgment debtor. (See NEC Electronics Inc. v. Hurt, 208 Cal. App. 3d 772, 778 (1989); Declaration of Jonich, 5-6; Declaration of Richardson in Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Judgment to Add Alter Ego Judgment Debtor, 3-9.) Plaintiff has not produced sufficient evidence to meet the multi-factor alter ego test that there was a unity of interest between Jeffrey Loomis and judgment debtor, and that inequitable results will follow if the corporate separateness is respected. (See Zoran Corp. v. Chen, 185 Cal. App. 4th 799, 811 (2010).) Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories, And For Order That The Truth Of The Matter Specified In Req.'S For Admissionbe Deemed Admitted, For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees, Costs, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Lawrence Miller, 650.592.9151, appearing for the Plaintiff Netline Corporation;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories, And For Order That The Truth Of The Matter Specified In Request's For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees, Costs, And For Sanctions is GRANTED. It is hereby ordered that the truth of the matters specified in Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions, Set Two are deemed admitted. In light of this ruling, Plaintiff's motion as it relates to its Form Interrogatories, Set Two, -- which only ask Form Interrogatory No. 17.0 - is moot, as Defendant's responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions, Set Two are now all deemed unqualified admissions. Defendant is further ordered to pay sanctions to Plaintiff in the amount of $935.00 (2.5 hours at $350.00 per hour plus $60.00 filing fee) on or before January 24, 2013. Order signed. Pro Tem Judge: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not reported. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lawrence Miller, Esq., Law Office, 650-592-9151, for plaintiff and moving party
Patrick M.K. Richardson, Esq., Law Office, 415-492-0535, for defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION's Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment.
Application for Right to Attach Order and Writ of Attachment is GRANTED. Plaintiff Netline Corporation submitted sufficient admissible evidence to establish its prima facie case, including evidence that Loomis Group acknowledged the debt. Defendant Loomis Group did not object to this evidence and failed to support its claimed defenses with admissible evidence. Plaintiff's objections the Declaration of Jeffrey Loomis Declaration are sustained. Plaintiff's application and supporting evidence are sufficient to satisfy the "probable validity" standard. Plaintiff to post a $10,000 bond. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
